description: Letter from Yefet to Abū l-Barakāt the physician, in Fustat (bāb qaṣr al-shamʿ). which the writer alludes to some bad news ("ever since I heard the news, I have fasted in the daytime and prostrated in bed") and urges the addressee to keep him informed. There is a mysterious word in Greek on verso written in a hand that may be modern: πιουται (piyyutim)?
tags: bedbound, illness letter 969-1517, fasting, illness: emotion, Arabic address, Illness
description_he: מכתב מיפת לאבו אל-ברכאת הרופא בפוסטאט, בו המחבר מרמז לחדשות רעות ומבקש לעדכנו. בצד ב' מילה מסתורית ביוונית: πιουται (פיוטים?).
